observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that the LCE direct the Cashier / Disbursing Officer to update,
compile and record on time the agency’s daily transactions on all the accounts
and funds in her official cashbooks, pursuant to paragraph 6 of COA Circular
No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

2.4 We recommended that Management:
a) Create an Inventory Committee composed of adequate number of members
to be able to complete the physical inventory in three months or less. The
Inventory Committee shall have at least one member each from the
Accounting and Property Divisions/Units of the agency,
b) Conduct physical count of all its PPE, whether acquired through purchase
or donation, including those constructed by administration and found at
station,
c) Submit the approved PIP to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar
days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities,
d) Upon completion of the physical count, the Inventory Committee shall
prepare the RPCPPE, using the prescribed format under the Government
Accounting Manual for National Government Agencies,
e) Ensure that the Property and Accounting Unit follows the rules outlined in
paragraphs 6.3.1 and 6.3.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January
31, 2020.

3.6 We recommended that Management:
a) Require all concerned agency officials/personnel to faithfully comply with
the pertinent rules and regulations on the submission of financial
reports/records including all supporting documents thereof within the
prescribed deadlines to ensure that all transactions are recorded in the
period when those transactions have transpired, in compliance with
Sections 100 and 122 of PD 1445.
b) Enforce the penal provision on withholding of salaries of responsible
officers in case of unjustified failure on their part to submit the reports on
time.

We commend the Municipal Accountant towards her efforts and commitment to submit the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements for Calendar Year 2021, pursuant to Section 74 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445 and Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We commend the management to continue the sound and effective practice on the remittance of the statutory contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of Republic Act (R.A.) 8291, otherwise known as the Government Service Insurance System Act, Section 3 (a) of Republic Act (R.A.) 9679 also known as the Home Development Mutual Fund Law and Section 20 (b) of Title III, Rule III of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the National Health Insurance Act (R.A.) 7875, as amended by Republic Act (R.A.) 9241.
observations.managementAction: management to continue the sound and effective practice on the remittance of the statutory contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of Republic Act (R.A.) 8291, otherwise known as the Government Service Insurance System Act, Section 3 (a) of Republic Act (R.A.) 9679 also known as the Home Development Mutual Fund Law and Section 20 (b) of Title III, Rule III of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the National Health Insurance Act (R.A.) 7875, as amended by Republic Act (R.A.) 9241, thus, the employees enjoy the privileges and benefits provided by the said agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management should follow the uniform property identification system for its Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) wherein a unique property number shall be assigned for each PPE item, in accordance with Paragraph 5.6 of COA Circular 2020-006, dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Philippines, paragraph 71 Application Guidelines (PAG) 4,5 and 6 of the Volume II Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard No. 17 - Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 124 and 57 of the Manual in the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the agency should require the Supply/Property Officer to secure his/her fidelity bonding from the Bureau of Treasury, in compliance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated April 25, 2019.b) Annual Investment Plan (AIP) that includes the programs/ projects to be funded from the proceeds of the loan. c.) General and Subsidiary Ledgers of the Loans Payable - Domestic Account.

We commend the management of insuring all the government properties with the General Insurance Fund (GIF) of the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS), pursuant to Republic Act (R.A) No. 656, otherwise known as Property Insurance Law.

a. Require all concerned agency officials/personnel to strictly comply with the pertinent rules and regulations on the submission of financial reports/records including all supporting documents thereof within the prescribed deadlines to ensure that all transactions are recorded in the period when those transactions have transpired, in compliance with Sections 100 and 122 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines; and b. Enforce the penal provision on withholding of salaries of responsible officers in case of unjustified failure on their part to submit the reports on time.
observations.managementAction: a. Require all concerned agency officials/personnel to strictly comply with the pertinent rules and regulations on the submission of financial reports/records including all supporting documents thereof within the prescribed deadlines to ensure that all transactions are recorded in the period when those transactions have transpired, in compliance with Sections 100 and 122 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines; b. Enforce the penal provision on withholding of salaries of responsible officers in case of unjustified failure on their part to submit the reports on time.Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; b.) transmitt immediately to the Audit Team the financial reports and unsubmitted disbursement vouchers as listed in Appendix P, otherwise, Notices of Suspension shall be issues; c.) Comply strictly with the timeframes on the rendition of accounts and preparation/submission of financial reports in accordance with section 7.2.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; d.) Imose appropriate sanctions against those officials/employees concerned who remained unmindful of the set deadline for the submission of the accounts/reports, pursuant to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management officials shall comply with documentary requirements and strictly follow the rules and regulations on the disbursement of funds with complete documentation to avoid suspension in audit, as prescribed pursuant to Section 9.1 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA) as prescribed under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We commend the Management for the implementation of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund for CY 2020 on the five percent (5%) of the total budget and thirty percent (30%) for Quick Response Fund (QRF) in accordance with NDRRMC -DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013, Section 21 of the Republic Act (RA) No. 10121, otherwise known as an Act strengthening the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction Management System, providing for the National Disaster Risk and Reduction and Management Plan, appropriating funds thereof and other purposes and Section 5.1.5 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We commend the LGU for the creation of a Municipal Waste Management Board pursuant to Section 12 of Republic Act (RA) No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to achieve a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program and ensure protection of public health and safety.

We commend the management for submission of the Annual Investment Program and the required Accomplishment Report to evaluate the viability of the projects undertaken, pursuant to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 to evaluate the viability of the projects undertaken.

We commend the management for submitting the GAD Plan, Quarterly Report on the utilization of GAD Fund and GAD Accomplishment Report, pursuant to Section 32 of the General Appropriation Act for Calendar Year 2021 , and Section V of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.

We commend the Chief Executive for instructing Accountable and directing the responsible Officers of the preparation and updating of reports, required records and registries to maintain updated data and information needed for audit and strengthen internal control in their respective divisions/offices.

We recommend that the management should follow the uniform property
identification number system for its Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)
wherein a unique property number shall be assigned for each PPE item, in
accordance with Paragraph 5.6 of COA Circular 2020-006, dated January
31, 2020.

We recommend that the agency should require the Supply/Property Officer
to secure his/her fidelity bonding from the Bureau of Treasury, in
compliance with Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated April 25, 2019.

6.6 We recommend that the Management:
a. Require all concerned agency officials/personnel to strictly comply with
the pertinent rules and regulations on the submission of financial
reports/records including all supporting documents thereof within the
prescribed deadlines to ensure that all transactions are recorded in the
period when those transactions have transpired, in compliance with
Sections 100 and 122 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise
known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines; and
b. Enforce the penal provision on withholding of salaries of responsible
officers in case of unjustified failure on their part to submit the reports on time.

7.6 We recommend that management officials shall comply with documentary
requirements and strictly follow the rules and regulations on the
disbursement of funds with complete documentation to avoid suspension
in audit, pursuant to Section 9.1 of the Rules and Regulations on the
Settlement of Accounts (RRSA) as prescribed under Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
We recommend that the municipality shall observe the provisions as prescribed under Section 182,187 and 189 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM), for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The responsible person followed the recommendation of the Audit team and begun applying the provisions as prescribed under Section 182,187 and 189 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM), for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer shall see to it that the Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Holder are reconciled for accurate recording and accounting and presentation in the financila statement.Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 17- Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).

observations.recommendation:
A.Instruct the in charge of the Office (ICO) - Municipal Treasurer, together with other concerned offices to (i) intensify the collection of real property taxes, form including other revenue sources to generate more income for the Municipality; (ii) post the notice of the deliquency once a week or for two consecutive weeks in a newspaper of general circulation of the Municipality.
b. Avail of the remedies provided under Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991 to enhance the collection of real property tax and enforce necessary administrative action thru levy on real property or judicial action as a last resort.
c. Remind the in charge of the office (ICO) - Municiapl Treasurer hod accountabilities in case of failure to collect, pursuant to Section 259 of the Republic Act (R.A) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction: A The LGU management had convened the Brgy. Officials of the concern Brgy. and the latter agreed to help disseminate information to delinquent taxpayers under their area of jurisdiction.
observations.reason: Follow up the LGU about the dissemination of information to delinquent taxpayers

observations.recommendation: We recommend the LGU to create Inventory Committee to conduct Physical Count of PPE and prepare Journal entries to effect the changes, pursuant to Section 63 of the Presidential Decree (P.D) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: A Inventory Committee was created and conduct Physical Count of PPE and prepare Journal entries to effect the changes, pursuant to Section 63 of the Presidential Decree (P.D) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines. Lecture on the concern was conducted; the Plans are still on the stage.
observations.reason: Follow up the LGU on the creation of the Inventory Committee and the execution of the Physical Count on PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management should do the following; 1.) send demand letter to the Lender (Bank). 2.) demand from the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS); 3) Upon receipt of the above documents, submit them to the Office of the Auditor together with the ff: a) Sangguniang Bayan Resolution approving the Loan, b) Annual Investment Plan (AIP) that includes the programs/projects to be funded from the proceeds of the loan; c) General and Subsidiary Ledgers of the Loans Payable - Domestic Account.
observations.managementAction: A The LGU-DOS management will send demand letter to the concern agencies. Upon receipts of the above documents, will submit them to the audit team together with the ff: a) Sangguniang Bayan Resolution approving the Loan, b) Annual Investment Plan (AIP) that includes the programs/projects to be funded from the proceeds og the loan. c.) General and Subsidiary Ledgers of the Loans Payable - Domestic Account.
observations.reason: Follow up the LGU on the status of the concern agencies they sent to the concern agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the LGU to adhere to paragraph 4.1 - 4.3 of the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 125 dated April 7, 2020 for posting and reporting and submit reports to the Audit Team such as Disbursement Vouchers (DVs), Disbursement Report of Check Vouchers Issued (RCISs) and other related reports.
observations.managementAction: A The utilization reports were duly posted in the LGU website on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to adhere to Section 7.1.1 and 7.2.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 and to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, including all Accountable Officers to: a.) Remind the Local Chief Executive (LCE) about his responsibility on all government funds, property required financial reports, pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; b.) Transmit immediately to the Audit Team the financial reports and unsubmitted disbursement vouchers as listed in Appendix P, otherwise, Notices of Suspension shall be issued; c.) Comply strictly with the timeframes on the rendition of accounts and preparation/submission of financial reports in accordance with section 7.2.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009; d.) Impose appropriate sanctions against those officials/employees concerned who remained unmindful of the set deadline for the submission of the accounts/reports, pursuant to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
observations.managementAction: A All activities were calendared to prevent the same occurrence. The management also set a cut-off date for every payment processing to give ample time to finance unit in the preparation of their monthly reports to COA.
observations.reason: The management cannot submit the DVs on the prescribed time.

observations.recommendation: Require the Municipal Engineer to submit inspection report of the projects under infrastructure services to faithfully discharge their functions and responsibilities as provided in the Local Government Code and the Joint Circular for maximum benefit.
observations.managementAction: A Municipal Engineer Engineer was reminded of its functions and responsibilities. The required documents will submit to COA.
observations.reason: The LGUs cannot submit Inspection Report on the prescribed time.

observations.recommendation:
We commend on the agency for their continuous good practice in preparing and
updating the Bank Reconciliation Statements on all its funds to facilitate our
immediate review and verification on the accounts.

2.2 We commend the management for its effort to enforce immediate settlement of
the audit suspensions totaling ₱ 2,066,053.57 in accordance with Section 9.1 of
COA Circular No. 94-001 dated January 20, 1994. Henceforth, ensure that
government auditing rules and regulations are strictly complied with before
processing claims/effecting payments to minimize suspensions and
disallowances in audit.

2.3 Informing by the Municipal Accountant to all persons responsible and/or
liable about the suspensions on their claim and require them to submit the
necessary documents and/or justification as required in the Notice of
Suspensions with a period of ninety (90) days from receipt of the notices;
otherwise the suspension shall mature into disallowance.

We commended the Management for being compliant with the regulations on
the deduction and timely remittance of premium contributions due to PhilHealth, BIR,
HDMF and GSIS in accordance to COA Memorandum No. 97-036.

We commend the Management for the implementation of the Local Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Fund for Calendar Year 2020 on the five
percent (5%) of the total budget and thirty percent (30%) for Quick Response
Fund (QRF) in accordance with Section 4.0 of the National Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council – Department of Budget and Management
– Department of Interior and Local Government (NDRRMC-DBM-DILG)
dated March 25, 2013 and Section 5.1.5 of the Commission on Audit (COA)
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Continue the efforts of the Municipality in promoting good planning, and budgetting
for GAD related activities in accordance with Sec. 4.4 of DBM - NEDA -NCRFW
Joint Circular No. 2004 -1.

We recommend that the municipality shall observe the provisions as prescribed
under Sections 182, 187 and 189 of the Government Accounting Manual (GAM),
for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I. The Municipal Accountant and
the Municipal Treasurer/General Services Officer shall see to it that the Supplies
Ledger Cards and Stock Holder are reconciled for accurate recording and
accounting and presentation in the financial statement.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. Instruct the In Charge of the Office (ICO) -Municipal Treasurer, together
with other concerned offices to (i) intensify the collection of real property
taxes, including other revenue sources to generate more income for the
Municipality; (ii) post the notice of the delinquency in publicly accessible and
conspicuous places in each barangay of the LGU concerned; and (iii)
publish the notice of delinquency once a week or for two consecutive weeks
in a newspaper of general circulation of the Municipality. Proper information
dissemination would help to inform concerned taxpayers of their tax
obligations and ultimately make them aware of their duty to pay in
accordance with Section 57 of Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 464, otherwise
known as the Real Property Tax Code.
b. Avail of the remedies provided under Section 256 of the Local Government
Code of 1991 to enhance the collection of real property tax and enforce
necessary administrative action thru levy on real property or judicial
action as a last resort.
c. Remind the In Charge of the Office (ICO) - Municipal Treasurer his
accountabilities in case of failure to collect, pursuant to Section 259 of the
Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code
of 1991.

We recommend the LGU to create Inventory Committee to to conduct Physical
Count of PPE and prepare journal entries to effect the changes, pursuant to
Section 63 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.) No. 1445, otherwise known as the
Government Auditing Code of the Philippines, paragraph 71 and Philippine
Application Guidance (PAG) 4,5 and 6 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting
Standard No. 17 - Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and Section 156 of the
Commission of Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and
Sections 124 and 57 of the Manual in the New Government Accounting System
(MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume I.

4.5 We recommend that the management should do the following:

4.5.1 Demand from the Lender - (Bank)
4.5.1.1. Abide to Commission on Audit (COA) Memorandum No. 2011 -005
dated March 23, 2011.
4.5.1.2 Copy of the Loan and Credit Financing Agreement.
4.5.1.3 Bank Debit Memos to effect the deduction from proceeds.
4.5.2 Demand from the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS)
4.5.2.1 Adhere to Commission on Audit (COA) Memorandum No. 2011-005
dated March 23, 2011.
4.5.2.2 Records of the Loan
4.5.3 Upon receipts of the above documents, submit them to the Office of the
Auditor together with the following:
4.5.3.1 Sangguniang Bayan Resolution approving the Loan
4.5.3.1 Annual Investment Plan (AIP) that includes the programs/ projects
to be funded from the proceeds of the loan
4.5.3.2 General and Subsidiary Ledgers of the Loans Payable - Domestic
Account

We recommend to the LGU to adhere to paragraph 4.1-4.3 of the Department of
Budget and Management (DBM) Local Budget Circular (LBC) No. 125 dated
April 7, 2020 and submit utilization reports to the Audit Team such as
Disbursement Vouchers (DVs), Report of Check Issued (RCIs) and other related
reports for audit purposes.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to adhere to Sections 7.1.1 and
7.2.1 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009 - 006 dated September
15, 2009 and to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal
Treasurer, including all Accountable Officers to:
a. Remind the Local Chief Executive (LCE) about his responsibility on all
government funds, property, required financial reports, pursuant to Section 7.1.1 of
the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15,
2009;
b. Transmit immediately to the Audit Team the financial reports and
unsubmitted disbursement vouchers as listed in Appendix P; otherwise,
Notices of Suspension shall be issued;
c. Comply strictly with the timeframes on the rendition of accounts and
preparation/submission of financial reports in accordance with Section 7.2.1
of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2009-006 dated September
15, 2009; and
d. Impose appropriate sanctions against those officials/employees concerned
who remained unmindful of the set deadline for the submission of the
accounts / reports, pursuant to Section 122 of the Presidential Decree (P.D.)
No. 1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the
Philippines.

Moreover, respective employees from various departments responsible for the
preparation of various reports had been cautioned by management regarding the
delayed submission thereof to the Commission on Audit (COA) as well as the non-
compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations governing the same.

Require the Municipal Engineer to submit inspection report of the projects under
infrastructure services to faithfully discharge their functions and responsibilities
as provided in the Local Government Code and the Joint Circular as above
mentioned for maximum benefit, pursuant to Section 477 (b) (3) (4) of the
Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of
1991.

We recommend that Management: Create an Inventory Team to conduct yearly physical inventory of PPE, in compliance with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992. Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting and property records for proper adjustment of the deficiencies if any to reflect the accurate balances of the PPE accounts. To facilitate with the reconciliation, the accountant must work. Maintain the PPLEC as provided in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume 1. Comply with pursuant to paragraph 8 of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard 17- Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE).
observations.managementAction: The Management: did not meet the time frame to conduct the yearly physical inventory of PPE, in compliance with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992. Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting and property records for proper adjustment of the deficiencies if any to reflect the accurate balances of the PPE accounts. The accountant and the GSO cannot cope up to absence of Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPLEC) and Property Card (PC). To facilitate with the reconciliation, the accountant must maintain the PPLEC as provided in Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume 1.
observations.reason: The recommendation was not among the prioritized activities for action by the management.

observations.recommendation:
1. Require the implementing offices to exert diligent efforts to implement the identified development projects in line with Section 287 of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160 and Section 2.3 of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, to ensure that the intended beneficiaries are not deprived of the benefits that the should have enjoyed.
2. Enjoin offices concerned to evaluate whether the projects and programs are implementable, otherwise revert to other more important with significant impact projects to the constituents or beneficiaries.
3. Require those involved in the formulation, development and implementation of programs/projects to faithfully discharge their functions and responsibilities as provided in the Local Government Code and the Joint Circular as above mentioned for maximum benefit.
observations.managementAction: The management acted on it by a campaign of tax drives to every communities in the Municipality.

observations.managementAction: The LGU remitted the statutory deductions however, the GSIS remained outstanding as of year end.
observations.reason: The Management remitted the statutory deductions, however, the GSIS remained outstanding as of year end.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all deductions due from the salaries of employees be remitted on time pursuant to Section 6(b) of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 8291, otherwise known as the Government Service Insurance Act of 1997, to avoid penalties, surcharges, and other pertinent charges. We also require the agency to submit strong justification, why they cannot remit on the time frame required.
observations.managementAction: Management settled the Notice of Suspensions issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management avail of the services provided by the LBP through an Automated Teller Machine (ATM) for payment of salaries to its permanent employees as well as casual and job order personnel in order to promote operational efficiency as well as strengthened internal control on cash and to avoid possible loss/misapplication.
observations.managementAction: The management reduced the number of employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU shall submit the disbursement vouchers on time for post audit examination. Continued disregard will be dealt with accordingly pursuant to the above stated provision of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the recommendation by submitting POs, copies of perfected contracts.
observations.reason: The management cannot submit the DVs on the prescribed time.

Continue the efforts of the Municipality in promoting good planning, and budgetting

observations.managementAction: The Management did not meet the time frame to conduct the physical count as committed.

observations.recommendation:
a. Abide to Section 57 of the Presidential Decree (PD) No. 464, otherwise known as the Real Property Tax Code.
b Conduct tax drives/ campaigns to every communities in the Municipality
c Extra effort to collect the Real Property Tax(RPT)
d Recommend to its Sanggunian Bayan to legislate an ordinance that would provide tax incentives to the real property owners who would pay early.
observations.managementAction: The management acted on it by A campaign of tax drives to every communities in the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all deductions such as Due to BIR/GSIS and PHILHEALTH withheld from the salaries of employees be remitted on time to avoid penalties, surcharges, and other charges. We also require the agency to submit strong justification why they cannot remit on the time frame required.
observations.managementAction: The LGU remitted the statutory deductions
observations.reason: however, the GSIS remained outstanding as of year end.

observations.recommendation:
Enforce immediate settlement of the audit suspensions totaling ₱266,715.87 in accordance with Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 94-001 dated January 20, 1994. Henceforth, ensure that government auditing rules and regulations are strictly complied with before processing claims/effecting payments to minimize suspensions and disallowances in audit.
The Municipal Accountant should inform all persons responsible and/or liable about the suspensions on their claim and require them to submit the necessary documents and/or justification as required in the Notice of Suspensions with a period of ninety (90) days from receipt of the notices; otherwise the suspension shall mature into disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Management settled the Notice ofSuspensions issued.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Chief Executive with the assistance of the Human Resource Development Office evaluate the necessity of some contractual/casuals in the different offices/departments of the LGU. If their services are no longer needed, they need not be renewed to save funds and resources.
observations.managementAction: The management reduced the number of employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to submit copies of POs, copies of perfected contracts and other supporting documents to the Auditor within the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to effect timely review of the perfected contracts and its supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the recommendation by submitting POs, copies of perfected contracts.

observations.recommendation:
1. Require the implementing offices to exert diligent efforts to implement the identified development projects in line with Section 287 of Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160 and Section 2.3 of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, to ensure that the intended beneficiaries are not deprived of the benefits that the should have enjoyed.
2. Enjoin offices concerned to evaluate whether the projects and programs are implementable, otherwise revert to other more important with significant impact projects to the constituents or beneficiaries.
3. Require those involved in the formulation, development and implementation of programs/projects to faithfully discharge their functions and responsibilities as provided in the Local Government Code and the Joint Circular as above mentioned for maximum benefit.
observations.managementAction: The LGU thru its Chief Executive require the different offices in charge to implement the development projects in line with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.reason: Some of the projects are not yet completed and are still on going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
 Create an Inventory Team to conduct the yearly physical inventory of PPE,
in compliance with Section 57, Volume II of the Local Government Accounting , with
the result thereof reported in proper format and substance, to check the
accountability ofproperty custodianship;
 Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting and property records for
proper adjustment of the deficiencies if any to reflect the accurate balances of the
PPE accounts. To facilitate reconciliation, the accountant must maintain the PPELC
as provided in Paragraphs b and d of Appendix 8, Volume II of the LGAS;
 Comply with PPSAS 17 by revising the Notes to Financial Statements to fully
disclose the required financial dataon PPE.

observations.recommendation: The LGU shall create or form a Municipal Waste Management Board pursuant to Section 12 of Republic Act (RA) No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to achieve a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program and ensure protection of public health and safety.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management avail of the services provided by the LBP through the LBP’s Automated Teller Machine (ATM) for automatic credit to the payment of salaries to its permanent employees as well as casual and job order personnel in order to promote operational efficiency as well as strengthened internal control on cash and to avoid further the risk of possible loss/misapplication.

observations.recommendation:
Enforce immediate settlement of the audit suspensions totaling ₱ 13,450,097.43 in accordance with Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 94-001 dated January 20, 1994.
Henceforth, ensure that government auditing rules and regulations are strictly complied with before processing claims/effecting payments to minimize suspensions and disallowances in audit.
The Municipal Accountant should inform all persons responsible and/or liable about the suspensions on their claim and require them to submit the necessary documents and/or justification as required in the Notice of Suspensions with a period of ninety (90) days from receipt of the notices; otherwise the suspension shall mature into disallowance.

Require the management to prepare and submit the LDRRMP/LDRRMFIP annually, aside from the 5-year Disaster Risk Reduction Plan (DRRP) for the years 2013-2018. The said plan shall present the 30% allocation for QRF in lump-sum and the 70% allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the particulars and details of programs and activities to be funded pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommend that the LGU shall submit the disbursement vouchers and other financial reports on the time frame as required for post audit and examination.
Continued disregard will be dealt with accordingly pursuant to the above stated provision of PD 1445.

We recommend the following:
1. Require the agency to fast track the implementation of on going projects to maximize benefits derived therefrom.
2. Require the Local Development Office to monitor implementation, and formulate a more sustainable projects that are implementable within the bound of the allotted budget as provided in the Local Government Code and the Joint Circular as above mentioned for maximum benefits of the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommend the agency to continue their implementation of the 5% GAD in accordance with Gender and Development (GAD) program/project in accordance with the guidelines provided in COA Memorandum No. 2009-020 dated May 15, 2009.

The LGU shall create or form a Municipal Waste Management Board pursuant to Section 12 of Republic Act (RA) No. 9003 or the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 to achieve a systematic, comprehensive and ecological solid waste management program and ensure protection of public health and safety.

To facilitate reconciliation, the accountant must maintain the PPELC as provided in Paragraphs b and d of Appendix 8, Volume II of the LGAS;  comply with PPSAS 17 by revising the Notes to Financial Statements to fully disclose the required financial data on PPE.
